title: Traffic control fixes — files, support, login
description:
After BASE vs SaaS session isolation shipped, legitimate users inside their own environment were getting HTTP 403 on every API call. Root causes: tenant SQL rejected membership users who still had legacy portal-users scope; BASE employees with any SaaS company link were treated as SaaS customers; Mike and Horizon admins were blocked on tenant subdomains needed for remote support.

Fixes keep the cross-environment wall (SaaS customers cannot use pipeshare.live, BASE portal-users cannot use tenant URLs) but allow: Mike and admin-panel users on any host; BASE employees on pipeshare.live; SaaS members with portal-users id on their company subdomain; correct BASE hosting tier for self-signup clients awaiting admin scope.

Who is affected: PipeShare BASE (pipeshare.live / OVH) and SaaS tenants (pipeshare.net subdomains). SaaS receives this when the next platform version is applied in cPanel releases.
changeLog: Allow portal-users scope in tenant membership SQL (fixes file explorer 403).
Treat BASE employees as BASE on pipeshare.live even with SaaS membership rows.
Allow Mike and Horizon admin overlay on tenant subdomains (remote support).
Pass admin fields through session environment checks.
Classify BASE self-signup clients as BASE until tenant scope is assigned.
